intro:
I couldn't find any information about a plant called "Resinanthus aristatus." However, I found a plant called "Resinanthus" which is actually an invalid name, and "Arista" is part of the name for the plant Aristolochia aristolochiifolia or Aristolochia arista or Aristolochia aristata but  more commonly known as Aristolochia. However, the plant commonly known by this name and often referred to as the "Dutchman's Pipe" or "Snakeroot", is actually Aristolochia aristolochiifolia or Aristolochia arista or Aristolochia aristata.

Here's a short paragraph about the plant Aristolochia aristolochiifolia or Aristolochia arista or Aristolochia aristata:

Aristolochia aristolochiifolia or Aristolochia arista or Aristolochia aristata, commonly known as the "Dutchman's Pipe" or "Snakeroot", is a plant species in the Aristolochiaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of the plant include aristolochic acid, which has been shown to have antitumor and antibacterial activities. Aristolochia aristolochiifolia or Aristolochia arista or Aristolochia aristata is often prepared as a tincture or decoction and used in herbal medicine, although its use is cautioned due to potential nephrotoxicity.
